svg.text: Split by value axis: within each behavioral value, does dot color (observed whole-run
move) match the background (the model's forecast 4-round move)?
one dot per run · 56 modelable 4-round runs from experiments/spread_util_unified.json, split by each run's value axis · 43 risk + 13 self-description = 56
Background = Δv_pred(4) = clip[−1,+1] of 4·ρ·σ — one selection step ρσ per round, wall-capped — identical in both
panels, on the dots' own endpoint-move color scale.
round-1 agreement ρ = corr(judge score, candidate value)
round-1 spread σ = within-prompt SD of value scores
23 of 29 movers match the forecast sign
round-1 agreement ρ = corr(judge score, candidate value)
12 of 12 movers match the forecast sign
match. All 6 discordant dots sit on the
match = sign of the move equals sign of
Scope: 4-round runs only — the 11 eight-round judge-schedule runs are excluded and 7 runs are skipped for an undefined round-1 ρ (zero within-pool
spread), out of 74 runs total, leaving 56. R = 4 is every plotted run's exact horizon. Mixed-pool runs also feel the outside-source pull (p − q), so
the 4·ρσ background is the self-only force map.
